cards.title: Anti-Inflammatory Diet
cards._legacyHtml:
Principle: Reduce dietary pro-inflammatory agents; increase anti-inflammatory nutrients to lower CRP, IL-6, TNF-alpha.
Include daily: Fatty fish (salmon, sardines, mackerel — 3x/week), turmeric (1 tsp + black pepper), ginger, dark leafy greens (spinach, kale), berries, walnuts, flaxseed (1 tbsp), olive oil (extra virgin), green tea (3 cups).
Avoid: Refined sugar, trans fats, vegetable/seed oils (sunflower, canola), refined flour, processed meats, alcohol, excess dairy.
Key nutrients: Omega-3 (EPA+DHA 2–4 g/day), curcumin 500–1000 mg, Vitamin D 2000 IU, Magnesium 300–400 mg, Zinc 15–25 mg.
Indicated for: Rheumatoid arthritis, IBD, eczema, asthma, metabolic syndrome, cardiovascular disease, autoimmune conditions.

cards.title: Elimination Diet — Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Purpose: Identify food intolerances and sensitivities triggering chronic symptoms.
Phase 1 — Elimination (3–4 weeks): Remove: gluten, dairy, eggs, soy, corn, peanuts, tree nuts, shellfish, nightshades, alcohol, caffeine, refined sugar. Eat: rice, sweet potato, most vegetables, most fruits, chicken, fish, legumes (if tolerated).
Phase 2 — Reintroduction (1 food every 3 days): Introduce one eliminated food. Observe symptoms for 72 hours before introducing next. Document reactions (skin, GI, mood, energy, joint pain).
Phase 3 — Maintenance: Build personalised diet excluding identified triggers.
Indicated for: IBS, eczema, chronic headache, fibromyalgia, ADHD, autoimmune conditions, unexplained fatigue.
Note: Nutritional supervision recommended. Calcium supplementation during dairy elimination.

cards.title: Ketogenic Diet — Clinical
cards._legacyHtml:
Macros: Fat 70–80%, Protein 15–20%, Carbohydrate 5–10% (<20–50 g/day net carbs).
Mechanism: Hepatic ketogenesis → beta-hydroxybutyrate → alternative fuel for brain, reduces glucose dependence, improves insulin sensitivity, anti-inflammatory.
Evidence-based indications: Epilepsy (refractory — Level 1A), T2DM reversal, obesity, PCOS, NAFLD, neurodegeneration (Alzheimer's — emerging).
Foods allowed: Meat, fish, eggs, hard cheese, butter, ghee, avocado, coconut oil, nuts (except cashews/peanuts), non-starchy vegetables.
Avoid: All grains, legumes, fruit (except berries in small amounts), starchy vegetables, sugar, alcohol.
Monitoring: Electrolytes (sodium, potassium, magnesium), lipid panel at 3 months, HbA1c. Watch for keto flu week 1 (headache, fatigue — increase electrolytes).
Duration: 3–6 months therapeutic. Cyclical ketogenic for long-term maintenance.

cards.title: Low-FODMAP Diet
cards._legacyHtml:
FODMAP: Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides And Polyols — short-chain carbohydrates that ferment in colon causing IBS symptoms.
High-FODMAP (avoid): Wheat, rye, onion, garlic, leek, asparagus, apples, pears, mango, watermelon, lactose (milk, soft cheese, yoghurt), honey, HFCS, cauliflower, mushrooms, legumes (most), cashews, pistachios.
Low-FODMAP (allowed): Rice, oats, quinoa, potatoes, carrots, cucumber, zucchini, spinach, tomato, banana (unripe), grapes, oranges, strawberries, hard cheese, lactose-free dairy, almonds (<10), eggs, meat, fish, tofu.
Protocol: Phase 1 (strict elimination 4–6 weeks) → Phase 2 (systematic reintroduction) → Phase 3 (personalised maintenance).
Evidence: 70–75% IBS patients respond. Gold standard for IBS management (Level 1A).
Note: Requires dietitian guidance. Reduce prebiotic diversity — not for long-term without reintroduction.

cards.title: Mediterranean Diet
cards._legacyHtml:
Key components: High olive oil, vegetables, fruits, legumes, whole grains, nuts, fish (especially oily). Moderate dairy, eggs, poultry. Low red meat, refined sugar, processed food.
Evidence (strongest in medicine): CVD risk ↓ 30% (PREDIMED trial). All-cause mortality ↓. Cognitive decline ↓. T2DM risk ↓ 19%. Depression risk ↓.
Daily targets: Olive oil ≥4 tbsp, vegetables ≥3 portions, fruit ≥3 portions, legumes ≥3 servings/week, fish ≥3 servings/week, nuts ≥3 servings/week.
Indian adaptation: Replace olive oil with cold-pressed sesame/coconut/groundnut oil. Replace red wine with amla juice. Include turmeric, ginger, fenugreek daily. Emphasise dal, rajma, chickpeas as legumes. Sabji-forward meals.
Indicated for: Cardiovascular disease prevention, metabolic syndrome, cognitive health, longevity, general wellness maintenance.

cards.title: Renal Diet — CKD Stages
cards._legacyHtml:
CKD Stage 3 (eGFR 30–60): Protein 0.8 g/kg/day. Potassium <2,000 mg/day. Phosphorus <800 mg/day. Sodium <2,300 mg/day. Avoid: bananas, oranges, tomatoes, potatoes (high K), dairy excess (high P), processed foods.
CKD Stage 4 (eGFR 15–30): Protein 0.6–0.8 g/kg/day. Potassium <1,500 mg/day. Phosphorus <600 mg/day. Fluid restriction if oedema. May need phosphate binders.
CKD Stage 5 / Dialysis: Protein ↑ 1.2 g/kg/day (dialysis removes protein). Strict potassium and phosphorus. Fluid restriction 1–1.5 L/day.
Leaching potassium from vegetables: Peel, cut small, boil in large water, discard water. Reduces K by 30–50%.
Naturopathic adjuncts (Stages 1–3): Punarnava decoction, Gokshura, alkaline diet (plant-heavy). Monitor eGFR 3-monthly.
Note: All renal diet modifications require nephrologist co-management.

cards.title: Diabetic Diet — Naturopathic
cards._legacyHtml:
Core principles: Low glycaemic index (GI <55), high fibre, controlled carbohydrates, avoid refined sugar and flour.
Meal timing: 3 main meals + 1–2 snacks. No meal skipping. Dinner before 7:30 PM. 12-hour overnight fast minimum.
Carbohydrate targets: 45–60 g/meal (moderate control). 30–45 g/meal (tight control). Count only net carbs.
Best foods: Methi seeds (soaked overnight, eat morning — ↓ FBS), bitter gourd juice (30 ml fasting), cinnamon (½ tsp/day), fenugreek, barley, oats, legumes, non-starchy vegetables.
Avoid: White rice in excess (replace with millets), refined flour, fruit juices, sugary beverages, deep-fried food, white bread.
Plate method: ½ plate non-starchy vegetables, ¼ plate protein, ¼ plate complex carb.
Key herbs (naturopathic adjuncts): Gymnema sylvestre 400 mg, Berberine 500 mg BD, Jamun seed powder 3 g, Karela 30 ml juice. Monitor HbA1c 3-monthly.

cards.title: Therapeutic Juicing — Clinical Guide
cards._legacyHtml:
Principles: Cold-press juicing preserves enzymes. Consume within 15 min of preparation. Morning on empty stomach for maximum benefit. Do not replace meals long-term — use as therapeutic adjunct.
Therapeutic combinations:
• Liver detox: Beetroot + carrot + ginger + lemon (100+100+1cm+½)
• Diabetes: Bitter gourd 30 ml + amla 30 ml + turmeric ½ tsp (fasting)
• Immunity: Wheatgrass 30 ml + amla 30 ml + ginger + lemon
• Hypertension: Celery + cucumber + beetroot + mint
• Gut healing: Aloe vera 30 ml + cabbage juice 100 ml (peptic ulcer)
• Anaemia: Pomegranate + beetroot + dates (blended)
Contraindications: CKD (high potassium in most juices), anticoagulants (avoid large amounts of dark leafy greens), allergy to ingredients.
Duration: Juice therapy 7–14 days as cleanse. Ongoing: 1 therapeutic juice/day as adjunct.
